body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

The scenario's User-observable signals are (1) the `ready â†’ working â†’ ready` state arc and (2) `TotalTokens > 0` after the turn. Pi supports the smallest possible round-trip natively: a single `pi --print -p <prompt>` invocation runs one assistant turn with no tool use and exits. Pi writes one JSONL session file per invocation under `~/.pi/agent/sessions/<cwd-slug>/<ts>_<UUID>.jsonl` immediately on launch (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy), so the daemon observes a `proc-<PID>` pre-session born ready, then a `transcript_new` + `state_transition`â†’ready for the UUID-keyed rollout session, then the workingâ†’ready flip as the single turn runs and completes, then `process_exited` at headless exit.

The pi parser identifies the assistant message end as a complete turn boundary: `stopReason=stop` on the final assistant event maps to `turn_done`, settling workingâ†’ready. This is the same turn-end detection the sibling session-start cell already exercises for the launch arc; basic-turn adds the workingâ†’ready turn on the rollout session. `TotalTokens > 0` is covered by the parser's per-turn token snapshot (not expressible in the phase DSL today; covered by parser unit tests).
caveats: Pi cannot accept a pre-assigned UUID â€” it generates one itself. The headless driver discovers the UUID from the session header on line 1 of the freshly-created transcript.

`pi --print` exits after the single turn, so the rollout session's terminal workingâ†’ready settle and the process_exited can land close together; the spec anchors turn_end to turn_start (not to session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the earlier birth ready.

sources.note: Identity.Name=pi, Source variant FilesUnderRoot under ~/.pi/agent/sessions/, ExactName{Name: "pi"} process matcher.
stopReason=stop on the final assistant event maps to turn_done (workingâ†’ready).
Headless `pi --print -p <prompt>` single-shot driver; resolves the freshly-created transcript and extracts the UUID from line 1.
